name: lox_kerosene_pressure_fed_6d2b99a6
description: Design a simple pressure-fed rocket feed system using liquid oxygen (LOX) and kerosene (RP-1) as propellants, with gaseous nitrogen as the pressurant gas. The system must include: (1) a nitrogen pressurant tank, (2) a LOX tank, (3) a kerosene tank, (4) nitrogen-to-LOX pressurization line, (5) nitrogen-to-kerosene pressurization line, (6) LOX feed line to injector, and (7) kerosene feed line to injector. FIXED quantities: propellants (LOX and kerosene), pressurant (gaseous nitrogen). TUNABLE quantities: tank pressures, tank volumes, line sizes, flow rates, and all component dimensions. The system must establish stable flow of both propellants without nonphysical warnings.

notes: System is pressure-fed - no turbopumps required
Nitrogen pressurizes both propellant tanks to drive flow
Ensure LOX and kerosene tanks maintain sufficient pressure for flow
LOX density ~1141 kg/m³ at cryogenic conditions
Kerosene density ~810 kg/m³ at room temperature
Nitrogen is gaseous pressurant at ambient or elevated temperature

checks.description: Simulation must complete without crashing or invalid configuration
System must establish non-zero mass flow through propellant lines
No nonphysical or degenerate warnings during simulation
Nitrogen tank must start with positive pressure (>1 bar absolute)
LOX tank must start with positive pressure (>1 bar absolute)
Kerosene tank must start with positive pressure (>1 bar absolute)
LOX feed line must have non-zero mass flow
Kerosene feed line must have non-zero mass flow
LOX tank mass must decrease (propellant consumed)
Kerosene tank mass must decrease (propellant consumed)
Nitrogen tank mass must decrease (pressurant consumed)
Simulation must run for at least 1 second to establish flow
